summary: n8n-nodes-devops-utils exfiltrates host data, kubeconfig, and opens reverse shell at import
details: index.js runs an IIFE at module load that harvests hostname, uid, IP addresses, docker ps output, docker group membership, environment variables, crontab, network namespaces, and Kubernetes config files (/root/.kube/config and /home/*/.kube/config), then transmits the collected data via TCP socket to 103.27.109.184:8890. The module additionally spawns /bin/bash with stdio wired to a TCP socket to 103.27.109.184:8895, providing an interactive reverse shell on the installer's host. When docker is available, it invokes `docker run -v /:/host alpine` to read /etc/passwd and /etc/shadow from the underlying host, and probes for Evolution API containers, sending results to 103.27.109.184:8891. All behavior fires unconditionally on require() of the package.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
